verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Wherefore, as the Holy Ghost saith, To-day if ye will hear His voice, harden not your hearts, as in the provocation in the day of temptation in the wilderness, when your fathers tempted Me, proved Me, and saw My works forty years.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Wherefore I was grieved with that generation, and said, They do alway err in their heart, and they have not known My ways.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So I sware in My wrath they shall not enter into My rest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Paul, having treated of hope, and having said that “We are His house, if we hold fast the confidence and the rejoicing of the hope firm unto the end” ( c. iii. ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 6 ); next shows that we ought to look forward with firmness, and he proves this from the Scriptures.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But be attentive, because he has expressed this in a manner somewhat difficult and not readily to be comprehended.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And therefore we must first make our own statements, and after we have briefly explained the whole argument, then make clear the words of the Epistle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For you will no longer need us, if you have understood the scope of the Apostle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: His discourse was concerning Hope, and that it behooves us to hope for the things to come, and that for those who have toiled here there will assuredly be some reward and fruit and refreshment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This then he shows from the prophet; and what says he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Wherefore as the Holy Ghost saith, To-day if ye will hear His voice, harden not your hearts, as in the provocation, in the day of temptation in the wilderness: when your fathers tempted Me, proved Me, and saw My works forty years.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Wherefore I was grieved with that generation, and said, they do alway err in their heart, and they have not known My ways.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So I sware in My wrath, they shall not enter into My rest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He says that there are “three” rests: one, that of the Sabbath, in which God rested from His works; the second, that of Palestine, into which when the Jews had entered they would be at rest from their hardships and labors; the third, that which is Rest indeed, the kingdom of Heaven; which those who obtain, do indeed rest from their labors and troubles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Of these three then he makes mention here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And why did he mention the three, when he is treating of the one only?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: That he might show that the prophet is speaking concerning this one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he did not speak (he says) concerning the first.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For how could he, when that had taken place long before?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Nor yet again concerning the second, that in Palestine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For how could he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he says, “They shall not enter into My rest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It remains therefore that it is this third.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But it is necessary also to unfold the history, to make the argument more clear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For when they had come forth out of Egypt, and had accomplished a long journey, and had received innumerable proofs of the power of God, both in Egypt, and in the Red Sea (cf.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 36 ), and in the wilderness, they determined to send spies to search out the nature of the land; and these went and returned, admiring indeed the country, and saying that it abounded in noble fruits, nevertheless it was a country of strong and invincible men: and the ungrateful and senseless Jews, when they ought to have called to mind the former blessings of God, and how when they were hemmed in the midst of the armies of so many Egyptians, He rescued them from their perils, and made them masters of their enemies’ spoils; and again, in the wilderness He clave the rock, and bestowed on them abundance of waters, and gave them the manna, and the other wonderful things which He wrought; [when they ought, I say, to have remembered this,] and to have trusted in God, they considered none of these things, but being struck with terror, just as if nothing had been done, they said, we wish to go back again into Egypt, “for God hath brought us out thither” (it is said) “to slay us, with our children and wives.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: God therefore being angry that they had so quickly cast off the memory of what had been done, sware that generation, which had said these things, should not enter into the Rest; and they all perished in the wilderness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: When David then, he says, speaking at a later period, and after these events, after that generation of men, said, “To-day, if ye will hear His voice, harden not your hearts,” that ye may not suffer the same things which your forefathers did, and be deprived of the Rest; he evidently [said this] as of some [future] rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if they had received their Rest (he says) why does He again say to them, “To-day if ye will hear His voice harden not your hearts,” as your fathers did?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What other rest then is there, except the kingdom of Heaven, of which the Sabbath was an image and type?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Next having set down the whole testimony (and this is, “To-day if ye will hear His voice, harden not your hearts, as in the provocation in the day of temptation in the wilderness, when your fathers tempted Me, proved Me, and saw My works forty years.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Wherefore I was grieved with that generation, and said, They do alway err in their heart, and they have not known My ways.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So I sware in My wrath, they shall not enter into My rest”), he then adds:

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Take heed, brethren, lest there be in any of you an evil heart of unbelief in departing from the living God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For from hardness unbelief ariseth: and as in bodies, the parts that have become callous and hard do not yield to the hands of the physicians, so also souls that are hardened yield not to the word of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it is probable besides that some even disbelieved as though the things which had been done were not true.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore he says, “Take heed lest there be in any of you an evil heart of unbelief in departing from the living God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For since the argument from the future is not so persuasive as from the past, he reminds them of the history, in which they had wanted faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if your fathers (he says) because they did not hope as they ought to have hoped, suffered these things, much more will you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Since to them also is this word addressed: for, “To-day” (he says) is “ever,” so long as the world lasts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Wherefore “exhort ye one another daily, while it is called to-day.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: That is, edify one another, raise yourselves up: lest the same things should befall you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Lest any one of you be hardened by the deceitfulness of sin.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou that sin produces unbelief?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For as unbelief brings forth an evil life, so also a soul, “when it is come into a depth of evils, becometh contemptuous” ( Prov. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 3 ), and having become contemptuous it endures not even to believe, in order thereby to free itself from fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For “they said” (one says), “The Lord shall not see, neither shall the God of Jacob regard.” ( Ps. xciv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again, “Our lips are our own: who is Lord over us?” ( Ps. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 4 ); and again “Wherefore hath the wicked man provoked God to wrath?” ( Ps. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 13 ); and again, “The fool hath said in his heart, there is no God; they are corrupt and become abominable in their doings.” ( Ps. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “There is no fear of God before his eyes, for he was deceitful before Him, to find out his iniquity and to hate.” ( Ps. xxxvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Yea and Christ also says this same thing, “Every one that doeth evil, hateth the light and cometh not to the light.” ( John iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then he adds ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 14 ), “For we have been made partakers of Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What is this, “We have been made partakers of Christ”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: We partake of Him (he means); we were made One, we and He—since He is the Head and we the body, “fellow-heirs and of the same body; we are one body, of His flesh and of His bones.” ( Eph. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 6; Rom. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “If we hold fast the beginning of our confidence [or, the principle of our subsistence] steadfast unto the end.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What is “the principle of our subsistence”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: The faith by which we stand, and have been brought into being and were made to exist, as one may say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Let us fear lest a promise being left us of entering into His rest, any of you should seem to come short of it; for to us was the Gospel preached as well as unto them when it is said, To-day if ye hear His voice” (for “To-day” is “at every time”).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then [he adds] “but the word of hearing did not profit them, as they were not mixed by faith with them that heard.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How did it not profit?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then wishing to alarm them, he shows the same thing by what he says:

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For some when they had heard did provoke, howbeit not all that came out of Egypt by Moses: And with whom was He grieved forty years?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Was it not with them that had sinned, whose carcasses fell in the wilderness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And to whom swear He that they should not enter into His rest, but to them that believed not?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So we see, that they could not enter in because of unbelief.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: After again repeating the testimony, he adds also the question, which makes the argument clear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he said (he repeats), “To-day if ye hear His voice, harden not your hearts, as in the provocation.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Of whom does he speak (he says) [as] having been hardened?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Of whom [as] not believing?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Is it not of the Jews?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Now what he says is to this effect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: They also heard, as we hear: but no profit came to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Do not suppose then that by “hearing” what is proclaimed ye will be profited; seeing that they also heard, but derived no benefit because they did not believe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Caleb then and Joshua, because they agreed not with those who did not believe, escaped the vengeance that was sent forth against them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And see how admirably he said, not, They did not agree, but, “they were not mixed”—that is, they stood apart, but not factiously when all the others had one and the same mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Here it seems to me that a faction too is hinted at.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For “we who have believed,” he says, “do enter into rest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: From what this is evident, he adds: “as He said, as I have sworn in My wrath, if they shall enter into My rest: although the works were finished from the foundation of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This indeed, is not evidence that we shall enter in, but that they did not enter in.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thus far he aims to show that as that rest does not hinder the speaking of another rest, so neither does this [exclude] that of Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Up to this point then, he wishes to show that they [the Israelites] did not attain to the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For because he means this, he says ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 4, 5 ), “For he spake in a certain place of the seventh day on this wise, And God did rest the seventh day from all His works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And in this place again, If they shall enter into My rest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest how that doth not hinder this from being a rest?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Seeing therefore it remaineth” (he says) “that some must enter therein, and they to whom it was first preached entered not in because of unbelief: again he limiteth a certain day, saying in David, To-day, after so long a time; as it has been said before.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But what is it that he means?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Seeing then” (he means) that “some must” certainly “enter in,” and “they did not enter in.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And that an entrance is proclaimed, and that “some must enter in,” let us hear from what this is clear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Because after so many years (he says) David again says: “To-day if ye will hear His voice, harden not your hearts” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 8 ), “For if Joshua had given them rest he would not afterward have spoken of another day.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It is evident, that he says these things, as of persons who are to attain some recompense.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “There remaineth therefore a rest for the people of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Whence [does this appear]?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: From the exhortation, “Harden not your hearts”: for if there were no rest, these exhortations would not have been given.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Neither would they have been exhorted not to do the same things [with the Jews] lest they should suffer the same things, unless they were about to suffer the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But how were they who were in possession of Palestine about to suffer the same things [i.e. exclusion from the rest] unless there were some other rest?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And well did he conclude the argument.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he said not rest but “Sabbath-keeping”; calling the kingdom “Sabbath-keeping,” by the appropriate name, and that which they rejoiced in and were attracted by.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For as, on the Sabbath He commands to abstain from all evil things; and that those things only which relate to the Service of God should be done, which things the Priests were wont to accomplish, and whatsoever profits the soul, and nothing else; so also [will it be] then.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: However it is not he who spoke thus, but what? ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 10 ), “For he that is entered into his rest, he also hath ceased from his own works, as God [did] from His.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: As God ceased from His works, he says, so he that hath entered into His rest [hath ceased].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For since his discourse to them was concerning rest, and they were desirous to hear when this would be, he concluded the argument with this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And [he said] “To-day,” that they might never be without hope.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Exhort one another daily,” he says, [“while it is called today,”] that is, even if a man have sinned, as long as it is “To-day,” he has hope: let no man then despair so long as he lives.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Above all things indeed, he says, “let there not be an evil heart of unbelief.” ( c. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But even suppose there should be, let no man despair, but let him recover himself; for as long as we are in this world, the “To-day” is in season.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But here he means not unbelief only, but also murmurings: “whose carcasses,” he says, “fell’ in the wilderness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then, lest any think that they will simply be deprived of rest only, he adds also the punishment, saying ( c. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 12 ), “For the Word of God is quick, and powerful; and sharper than any two-edged sword, and pierceth even to the dividing asunder of soul and spirit, and of the joints and marrow: and is a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Here he is speaking of Hell and of punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “It pierceth” (he says) into the secrets of our heart, and cutteth asunder the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Here it is not the falling of carcasses nor, as there, the being deprived of a country, but of a heavenly kingdom; and being delivered to an everlasting hell, and to undying punishment and vengeance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “But exhort one another.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Observe the gentleness and mildness [of the expression]: he said not “Rebuke,” but “Exhort.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thus we are required to bear ourselves towards those who are straightened by affliction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This he says also in writing to the Thessalonians, “Warn them that are unruly” ( 1 Thess. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 14 ), but in speaking of the feeble-minded, not so, but what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Comfort the feeble-minded, support the weak, be patient toward all men”; that is, do not cease to hope; do not despair.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he that does not encourage one who is straightened by affliction, makes him more hardened.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Lest any of you,” he says, “be hardened by the deceitfulness of sin.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He means either the deceit of the devil (for it is indeed a deceit, not to look for the things to come, to think that we are without responsibility, and that we shall not pay the penalty for our deeds here, neither will there be a resurrection); or in another sense insensibility [or] despairing is deceit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For to say, ‘What is there left?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: I have sinned once for all, I have no hope of recovering myself,’ is deceit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then he suggests hopes to them, saying ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 14 ), “We are made partakers of Christ”; All but saying, He that so loved us, He that counted us worthy of so great things, as to make us His Body, will not suffer us to perish.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us consider (he says) of what we have been thought worthy: we and Christ are One: let us not then distrust Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again, he hints at that which had been said in another place, that “If we suffer, we shall also reign with Him.” ( 2 Tim. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For this is [implied in] “We are made partakers,” we partake of the same things whereof Christ also partakes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He urges them on from the good things; “for we are,” he says, “partakers of Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then, again, from gloomy ones ( c. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 1 ), “Let us fear, lest at any time a promise being left us of entering into His rest, any of you should seem to come short of it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For that is manifest and confessed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “They proved Me,” He says, “and saw My works forty years.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou that it is not right to call God to account, but whether He defend [our cause] or not, to trust Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For against those [of old] he now brings this charge, that “they tempted God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he that will have proof either of His power, or of His providence, or of His tender care, does not yet believe, either that He is powerful or kind to man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This he hints also in writing to these [Hebrews] who probably already wished, in their trials, to obtain experience and positive evidence of His power and His providential care for them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest that in all cases the provocation and the angering arises from unbelief.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What then does he say? ( c. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “There remaineth therefore a rest for the people of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And see how he has summed up the whole argument.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “He sware,” saith he, to those former ones, “that they should not enter into” the “rest,” and they did not enter in.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then long after their time discoursing to the Jews, he says, “Harden not your hearts,” as your fathers, showing that there is another rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For of Palestine we have not to speak: for they were already in possession of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Nor can he be speaking of the seventh [day]; for surely he was not discoursing about that which had taken place long before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It follows therefore that he hints at some other, that which is rest indeed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For that is indeed rest, where “pain, sorrow and sighing are fled away” ( Isa. xxxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 10 ): where there are neither cares, nor labors, nor struggle, nor fear stunning and shaking the soul; but only that fear of God which is full of delight.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: There is not, “In the sweat of thy face thou shalt eat thy bread,” nor “thorns and thistles” ( Gen. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 19, 18 ); no longer, “In sorrow thou shalt bring forth children, and to thy husband shall be thy desire and he shall rule over thee.” ( Gen. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: All is peace, joy, gladness, pleasure, goodness, gentleness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: There is no jealousy, nor envy, no sickness, no death whether of the body, or that of the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: There is no darkness nor night; all [is] day, all light, all things are bright.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It is not possible to be weary, it is not possible to be satiated: we shall always persevere in the desire of good things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Would you that I should also give you some image of the condition there?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It is impossible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But yet, so far as it is possible, I will try to give you some image.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us look up into the heaven when without any intervening cloud it shows forth its crown [of stars].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then when we have dwelt long on the beauty of its appearance, let us think that we too shall have a pavement, not indeed such [as this], but as much more beautiful as the gold is than the clay, and [let us think] on the higher roof which is again beyond; then on the Angels, the Archangels, the infinite multitude of unbodied powers, the very palace of God itself, the Throne of the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But language is too weak (as I said) to set forth the whole.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Experience is necessary, and the knowledge which [cometh] by experience.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Tell me, how was it (think you) with Adam in Paradise?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This course of life is far better than that, as much as heaven [is better] than earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But however let us search after another image still.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If it happened that he who now reigns was master of the whole world, and then was troubled neither by wars nor by cares, but was honored only and lived delicately; and had large tributes, and on every side gold flowed in to him, and he was looked up to, what feelings do you think he would have, if he saw that all the wars in all parts of the world had ceased?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Something such as this will it be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But rather I have not even yet arrived at that image [which I seek]; therefore I must search after another too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Consider then, I pray you: for as some royal child, so long as he is in the womb, has no sense of anything, but should it happen that he suddenly came forth from thence, and ascended the royal throne, not gradually, but all at once received possession of all things; so is it as regards this [present] and that [future] state.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Or, if some captive, having suffered innumerable evils, should be caught up at once to the royal throne.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But not even thus have I attained to the image exactly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For here indeed whatever good things a person may obtain, even shouldst thou say the kingdom itself, during the first day indeed his desires are in full vigor, and for the second too, and the third, but as time goes on, he continues indeed to have pleasure, but not so great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For whatever it be, it always ceases from familiarity with it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But yonder it not only does not diminish, but even increases.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For consider how great a thing it is, that a soul after departing thither, should no longer look for an end of those good things, nor yet change, but increase, and life that has no end, and life set free from all danger, and from all despondency and care, full of cheerfulness and blessings innumerable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if when we go out into a plain, and there see the soldiers’ tents fixed with curtains, and the spears, and helmets, and bosses of the bucklers glittering, we are lifted up with wonder; but if we also chance to see the king himself running in the midst or even riding with golden armor, we think we have everything; what thinkest thou [it will be] when thou seest the everlasting tabernacles of the saints pitched in heaven?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: (For it is said, “They shall receive you into their everlasting tabernacles”— Luke xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 9 ) when thou seest each one of them beaming with light above the rays of the sun, not from brass and steel, but from that glory whose gleamings the eye of man cannot look upon?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And this indeed with respect to the men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But what, if one were to speak of the thousands of Angels, of Archangels, of Cherubim, of Seraphim, of thrones, of dominions, of principalities, of powers, whose beauty is inimitable, passing all understanding?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But how far shall I go in pursuing what cannot be overtaken?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For eye hath not seen,” it is said, “nor ear heard, neither have entered into the heart of man, the things which God hath prepared for them that love Him.” ( 1 Cor. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore nothing is more pitiable than those who miss, nor anything more blessed than those who attain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us then be of the blessed, that we may attain to the everlasting good things that are in Christ Jesus our Lord, with whom to the Father together with the Holy Ghost be glory, might, honor, now and for ever and world without end.
